rootCause: Running npm from frontend on Windows PowerShell did not inherit the root peer-resolution guard, so stale local node_modules state triggered ERESOLVE and prevented prism-svelte from being installed, which then broke astro.config.mjs import during npm run dev.
resolution: Added frontend/.npmrc with legacy-peer-deps=true so frontend-scoped npm installs resolve peers consistently on local Windows shells, then added a regression test that enforces the setting remains present.
